skill.name: dotnet-backend
skill.description: Build ASP.NET Core 8+ backend services with EF Core, auth, background jobs, and production API patterns.
skill.summary: Build ASP.NET Core 8+ APIs with EF Core, authentication, background workers, and production backend patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Ruby backtick execution, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance were false positives caused by fenced C# examples and ordinary API text. The skill does request Bash plus file editing tools, so publication is acceptable with a medium-risk warning for broad command and filesystem capability.

medium_findings.title: Broad Tool Access Requires User Oversight
medium_findings.description: The skill metadata allows Read, Write, Edit, and Bash. This is useful for backend implementation work, but Bash can run arbitrary local commands when an assistant uses the skill.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration explicitly includes Bash and file modification tools. No malicious command is present, but the capability is materially risky for a community coding skill.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Execution Findings Are Fenced C# Examples
low_findings.description: The flagged locations are Markdown code fence delimiters around C# examples for ASP.NET Core, EF Core, JWT, and background services. They are not Ruby shell backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each cited location is a Markdown code fence line or the start of a fenced C# sample. There is no Ruby code or shell command execution syntax in those locations.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Are Prose Matches
low_findings.description: The flagged lines describe ASP.NET Core backend work and EF Core data access. They do not contain weak cryptographic algorithms or insecure cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is skill description and use-case prose, not cryptographic code. The visible JWT sample uses HmacSha256, which is not the weak algorithm alleged by the static finding.

low_findings.title: Static System Reconnaissance Finding Is API Routing Code
low_findings.description: The flagged line returns a CreatedAtAction response in a controller example. It does not collect host, user, process, or network reconnaissance data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code at the cited line is a standard ASP.NET Core controller response helper. No system metadata access or reconnaissance behavior is present.

content.value_statement: ASP.NET Core teams need reliable patterns for APIs, data access, auth, and background work. This skill guides backend implementation with practical .NET 8+ examples.
actual_capabilities: Designs ASP.NET Core 8+ Minimal API and controller-based endpoints.
Guides EF Core DbContext setup, migrations, query patterns, and DTO projection.
Provides authentication and authorization patterns using JWT and ASP.NET Core policies.
Explains background worker patterns with scoped services and scheduled job options.
Recommends testing, validation, logging, health checks, and OpenAPI practices.
limitations: Focuses on backend services and does not cover frontend application development.
Assumes modern ASP.NET Core 8+ patterns, not legacy .NET Framework applications.
Cloud deployment guidance is general unless the user provides provider details.
Generated code still needs project-specific review, testing, and secret management.

use_cases.title: Create a New API Service
use_cases.description: Start a backend with endpoints, validation, persistence, authentication, and OpenAPI documentation.

use_cases.title: Modernize Data Access
use_cases.description: Refactor EF Core queries, DTO mapping, migrations, and read-only query paths for maintainability.

use_cases.title: Add Reliable Background Work
use_cases.description: Implement worker services, scoped dependency usage, scheduled jobs, and retry-aware processing.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Endpoint
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a small API feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an ASP.NET Core 8 endpoint for managing products. Include validation, DTOs, EF Core persistence, and clear error responses.

prompt_templates.title: Add Authentication
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for secure access control.
prompt_templates.prompt: Add JWT authentication and role-based authorization to this ASP.NET Core API. Explain required configuration and testing steps.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor EF Core Queries
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for data access improvements.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this EF Core repository pattern and improve query performance, tracking behavior, DTO projection, and migration safety.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Production Backend
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for system design and implementation planning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a production ASP.NET Core backend for order processing with APIs, EF Core, background jobs, auth, logging, health checks, and tests.

output_examples.input: Create a user registration API with password hashing and validation.
output: A structured endpoint plan with request and response models.
Guidance for password hashing, validation, persistence, and created responses.
Testing notes for invalid input, duplicate users, and successful registration.

output_examples.input: Improve a slow EF Core query used by an admin dashboard.
output: A diagnosis of tracking, projection, filtering, and eager loading issues.
Recommended query changes using read-only patterns and targeted DTOs.
Verification steps using logging, query plans, and realistic test data.

output_examples.input: Add a background email sender to an existing service.
output: A worker design that uses scoped services correctly.
Recommendations for batching, cancellation, logging, and failure handling.
Operational checks for retries, duplicate sends, and graceful shutdown.

best_practices: Provide project structure, target .NET version, database provider, and authentication requirements.
Ask for tests and migration guidance when changing persistence or security behavior.
Review generated configuration for secrets, connection strings, logging, and environment-specific settings.
anti_patterns: Do not paste production secrets, signing keys, or connection strings into prompts.
Do not accept generated migrations or auth changes without reviewing the final diff.
Do not use the skill for frontend-only work or legacy .NET Framework assumptions.

faq.question: Does this skill generate complete ASP.NET Core projects?
faq.answer: It can help design and implement project pieces, but final integration depends on your repository structure.

faq.question: Can it work with both Minimal APIs and controllers?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes guidance for Minimal APIs and controller-based ASP.NET Core services.

faq.question: Does it handle authentication and authorization?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers JWT, ASP.NET Core Identity, OAuth concepts, and policy-based authorization patterns.

faq.question: Can it optimize EF Core performance?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest projection, tracking, eager loading, migrations, and query review practices.

faq.question: Is this skill safe for production code?
faq.answer: It provides production-oriented patterns, but generated changes require code review, tests, and secret handling checks.

faq.question: Why is the security risk rated medium?
faq.answer: The skill declares Bash and file editing access. That is normal for coding work but requires user oversight.
